perturbation.original_text: Customer agrees to pay the fees and other charges for the Hosting Services and other services provided under this Agreement as specified in Exhibit A of this Agreement. CUSTOMER AGREES TO PAY FOR HOSTING SERVICES ON OR BEFORE THE FIRST DAY OF THE MONTH IN WHICH THE HOSTING SERVICES ARE PROVIDED, except that, with respect to Additional Fees (as defined in Exhibit A), eGain will invoice Customer for such Fees in the month after the month in which such fees accrue as provided in Exhibit A.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer agrees to pay the fees and other charges for the Hosting Services and other services provided under this Agreement as specified in Exhibit A of this Agreement. CUSTOMER AGREES TO PAY FOR HOSTING SERVICES WITHIN 90 DAYS OF RECEIPT OF INVOICE, unless otherwise specified in Exhibit A. In the event of a dispute, Customer may withhold payment until the dispute is resolved, but any undisputed amount must be paid within 90 days.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the payment terms introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, payment was due on or before the first day of the month in which services were provided. Now, the payment term is extended to within 90 days of invoice receipt, unless specified in Exhibit A, creating uncertainty about the specific payment schedule. Additionally, the condition allowing withholding payment during a dispute further undermines the initial payment agreement, contradicting the obligation to pay within a fixed timeframe. The contradicted_text showcases the initial statement about monthly payments which conflicts with the updated payment terms
perturbation.contradicted_text: Customer agrees to pay the fees and other charges for the Hosting Services and other services provided under this Agreement as specified in Exhibit A of this Agreement. CUSTOMER AGREES TO PAY FOR HOSTING SERVICES ON OR BEFORE THE FIRST DAY OF THE MONTH IN WHICH THE HOSTING SERVICES ARE PROVIDED, except that, with respect to Additional Fees (as defined in Exhibit A), eGain will invoice Customer for such Fees in the month after the month in which such fees accrue as provided in Exhibit A. All amounts payable hereunder are exclusive of any and all taxes, and Customer is responsible for payment of such taxes (excluding taxes based on eGain's net income). All prices are stated, and Customer shall pay, in United States dollars. Payment received by eGain after the due date shall be subject to a late fee equal to one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month, or, if less, the maximum amount allowed by applicable law. At the end of the initial one-year term of this Agreement and any subsequent one-year terms, eGain may adjust the monthly fee payable under this Agreement by providing Customer written notice of such adjustment at least sixty (60) days prior to the beginning of the new term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by altering the payment terms. The original text and contradicted text state that payment for hosting services is due on or before the first day of the month. The changed text extends this to within 90 days of invoice receipt, unless otherwise specified in Exhibit A. The new payment terms create a direct conflict with the original requirement of payment on or before the first of the month, leading to an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', because now there conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall continue in effect from the Effective Date for a one (1) year period, unless earlier terminated as set forth below, and thereafter shall renew automatically for successive one (1) year periods unless either party gives the other party at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of its intent not to renew the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue in effect from the Effective Date for a one (1) year period, unless earlier terminated as set forth below, and thereafter shall renew automatically for successive one (1) year periods unless either party gives the other party at least ninety (90) days prior written notice of its intent not to renew the Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, eGain may terminate the agreement at any time with 30 days notice
perturbation.explanation: The modification to the renewal term introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, either party could prevent automatic renewal with 30 days' notice. Now, Customer has to provide 90 days' notice, while eGain retains right to terminate with 30 days notice, creating an in-text contradiction on the termination requirements and favoring one party over the other. The contradicted_text highlights the original contract termination clause that is contradicted due to the imposed change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term and Termination. This Agreement shall continue in effect from the Effective Date for a one (1) year period, unless earlier terminated as set forth below, and thereafter shall renew automatically for successive one (1) year periods unless either party gives the other party at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of its intent not to renew the Agreement. In addition, either party may terminate this Agreement by giving to the other party written notice eGAIN COMMUNICATIONS CORPORATION HOSTING AGREEMENT of such termination upon the other party's material breach of any material term (subject to the other party's right to cure within thirty (30) days after receipt of such notice), the other party's insolvency, or the institution of any bankruptcy or similar proceedings by or against the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the notice period required for termination. Originally, both parties needed to give 30 days' notice to prevent automatic renewal. The modification changes this, requiring the Customer to give 90 days' notice while eGain retains the right to terminate with only 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the original clause in the contradicted text which states: "...thereafter shall renew automatically for successive one (1) year periods unless either party gives the other party at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of its intent not to renew the Agreement." This creates an inconsistency in the termination requirements, thus causing a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: eGain shall be entitled to retain a copy (whether electronic or otherwise) of the Online Messages and the Reports for its records and internal purposes and shall not disclose such Online Messages or Reports to any third party except as permitted under Section 4.
perturbation.changed_text: eGain shall be entitled to retain a copy (whether electronic or otherwise) of the Online Messages and the Reports for its records and internal purposes and may disclose such Online Messages or Reports to third parties for any legitimate business purpose, notwithstanding Section 4.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated that eGain could not disclose Online Messages or Reports to any third party except as permitted under Section 4, establishing a clear confidentiality restriction. The modified version contradicts this by allowing disclosure to third parties for any legitimate business purpose, regardless of Section 4. This creates significant uncertainty and directly contradicts the initial confidentiality agreement. The contradicted_text indicates the original confidentiality of the online messages, contradicting the modified right to disclose to third parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Effect of Termination. Upon any termination of this Agreement, eGain shall immediately cease providing all Hosting Services, and Customer shall no longer have access to the Software or the eGain System. Except in the event of termination for Customer's breach, eGain shall provide Customer with an electronic copy of the final Reports (covering the month just prior to termination of this Agreement). eGain shall be entitled to retain a copy (whether electronic or otherwise) of the Online Messages and the Reports for its records and internal purposes and shall not disclose such Online Messages or Reports to any third party except as permitted under Section 4. Within fifteen (15) days of any termination of this Agreement, Customer shall pay to eGain all unpaid fees accrued prior to termination. Sections 4, 5 (as to amounts accrued but unpaid), 7, 8, 10.2 and 12 and Exhibit A (as to amounts accrued but unpaid) shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits the disclosure of Online Messages or Reports to any third party except as permitted under Section 4. The changed text allows disclosure to third parties for any legitimate business purpose, regardless of Section 4. This directly contradicts the original restriction and creates a strong in-text contradiction within the document. Therefore, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, which states that eGain 'shall not disclose such Online Messages or Reports to any third party except as permitted under Section 4.'
